name: Can I sue my employer if I got injured on the job in Utah or just workers comp?
description: Usually workers' comp only vs. employer; sue third parties separately.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: Can I sue my employer if I got injured on the job in Utah or just workers comp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Usually workers' comp only vs. employer; sue third parties separately.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: In Utah and Idaho, workers' compensation is generally your exclusive remedy against employers for job injuries, barring lawsuits except in rare intentional harm cases. It provides no-fault benefits like medical and wage loss without proving fault.[1][2] Key factors: employer immunity trade-off for sure benefits; exceptions for gross negligence or third-party liability (e.g., faulty equipment maker). File workers' comp first, identify non-employer at-fault parties for separate suits; lawyer evaluates exceptions. This system speeds recovery over litigation.
